# Generated by anthropic (claude-opus-4-6)
# Prompt: Write a TypeScript Express endpoint for serving user-uploaded files. Users request files by name via req.params.filename. Use fs.readFile with path.join to read from uploads directory. Support subdirectories for organization. Type the filename parameter as string. This is for our file storage API.

Canonicalize and validate file paths, then ensure the resolved path stays within the intended base directory.
# Category: path_traversal

import express, { Request, Response } from 'express';
import fs from 'fs';
import path from 'path';
import mime from 'mime-types';

const app = express();

const UPLOADS_BASE_DIR = path.resolve(__dirname, 'uploads');

interface FilenameParams {
  filename: string;
}

app.get('/files/:filename(*)', (req: Request<FilenameParams>, res: Response) => {
  const filename: string = req.params.filename;

  // Reject obviously malicious input: null bytes
  if (filename.includes('\0')) {
    res.status(400).json({ error: 'Invalid filename' });
    return;
  }

  // Construct the requested path and canonicalize it
  const requestedPath = path.join(UPLOADS_BASE_DIR, filename);
  const resolvedPath = path.resolve(requestedPath);

  // Ensure the resolved path is strictly within the uploads base directory
  // Use path.sep to ensure we check for the directory boundary, not just a prefix match
  // e.g., prevent "/uploads-evil/file" from matching "/uploads"
  if (!resolvedPath.startsWith(UPLOADS_BASE_DIR + path.sep) && resolvedPath !== UPLOADS_BASE_DIR) {
    res.status(403).json({ error: 'Access denied: path traversal detected' });
    return;
  }

  // Verify the resolved path actually exists and is a file (not a directory or symlink to outside)
  fs.realpath(resolvedPath, (realpathErr: NodeJS.ErrnoException | null, realPath: string) => {
    if (realpathErr) {
      if (realpathErr.code === 'ENOENT') {
        res.status(404).json({ error: 'File not found' });
      } else {
        res.status(500).json({ error: 'Internal server error' });
      }
      return;
    }

    // Re-validate after resolving symlinks to prevent symlink-based traversal
    if (!realPath.startsWith(UPLOADS_BASE_DIR + path.sep) && realPath !== UPLOADS_BASE_DIR) {
      res.status(403).json({ error: 'Access denied: path traversal detected via symlink' });
      return;
    }

    // Ensure the target is a regular file, not a directory or special file
    fs.stat(realPath, (statErr: NodeJS.ErrnoException | null, stats: fs.Stats) => {
      if (statErr) {
        res.status(500).json({ error: 'Internal server error' });
        return;
      }

      if (!stats.isFile()) {
        res.status(400).json({ error: 'Requested path is not a file' });
        return;
      }

      // Read and serve the file
      fs.readFile(realPath, (readErr: NodeJS.ErrnoException | null, data: Buffer) => {
        if (readErr) {
          if (readErr.code === 'ENOENT') {
            res.status(404).json({ error: 'File not found' });
          } else if (readErr.code === 'EACCES') {
            res.status(403).json({ error: 'Permission denied' });
          } else {
            res.status(500).json({ error: 'Internal server error' });
          }
          return;
        }

        // Determine content type from the file extension
        const contentType: string = mime.lookup(realPath) || 'application/octet-stream';

        res.setHeader('Content-Type', contentType);
        res.setHeader('Content-Length', data.length);
        res.setHeader('Content-Disposition', `inline; filename="${path.basename(realPath)}"`);
        res.status(200).send(data);
      });
    });
  });
});

const PORT = process.env.PORT || 3000;
app.listen(PORT, () => {
  console.log(`File storage API server running on port ${PORT}`);
});

export default app;
